assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable gives rise to foreign currency exposure in the consolidated financial statements.
The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 at the consolidated level.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a formal hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability with profit or loss exposure.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a formal hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items and forecast transactions, including intragroup contexts discussed in the provided extracts.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct foreign currency hedge accounting model at the consolidated level.
